ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) encryptor execution via Rust binary --access-token / --child / --propagated CLI flags
ucs.description: Hunts the BlackCat/ALPHV Rust-based encryptor by its mandatory command-line flags. The binary refuses to run without `--access-token=<32-byte hex>` (acts as a license/mutex check unique to this RaaS family) and operators commonly chain it with `--child`, `--paths`, `--prop-file`, `--propagated`, `--no-prop-servers`, `--safeboot`, or `--drag-and-drop` — none of which appear in any legitimate Windows binary. CrowdStrike tracks this actor as SCATTERED SPIDER's preferred RaaS (also Noberus per Symantec/Mandiant).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND (Processes.process="*--access-token=*" OR (Processes.process="*--access-token *" AND (Processes.process="*--child*" OR Processes.process="*--paths*" OR Processes.process="*--prop-file*" OR Processes.process="*--propagated*" OR Processes.process="*--no-prop-servers*" OR Processes.process="*--safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*--drag-and-drop*"))) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// BlackCat/ALPHV Rust encryptor mandatory flag — 32-byte hex access token unique to this family
| where ProcessCommandLine has "--access-token"
// Strengthen with any of the operator-chain flags the binary exposes
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "--child", "--paths", "--prop-file", "--propagated",
    "--no-prop-servers", "--no-prop", "--no-net",
    "--no-vm-kill", "--safeboot", "--safeboot-instance",
    "--drag-and-drop", "--extra-verbose", "--logs"
  )
| extend AccessToken = extract(@"--access-token[= ]([A-Fa-f0-9]{32,})", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine, AccessToken,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
// BlackCat/ALPHV Rust encryptor mandatory access-token argument
| where CommandLine has "--access-token"
| where CommandLine has_any (
    "--child", "--paths", "--prop-file", "--propagated",
    "--no-prop-servers", "--no-prop", "--no-net",
    "--no-vm-kill", "--safeboot", "--safeboot-instance",
    "--drag-and-drop", "--extra-verbose", "--logs"
  )
| extend AccessToken = extract(@"--access-token[= ]([A-Fa-f0-9]{32,})", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, AccessToken, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackCat/ALPHV Ransomware Encryptor CLI Flags
id: 6c8a4d9e-3b41-4f2c-a6e7-8b9c1d2f3e4a
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of the BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) Rust-based ransomware encryptor identified by its mandatory --access-token flag combined with operator chain flags (--child, --paths, --propagated, --safeboot, etc.)
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/06/13/the-many-lives-of-blackcat-ransomware/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-353a
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/noberus-blackcat-alphv-rust-ransomware
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1564.006
  - attack.t1490
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_token:
    CommandLine|contains: '--access-token'
  selection_chain:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--child'
      - '--paths'
      - '--prop-file'
      - '--propagated'
      - '--no-prop-servers'
      - '--no-prop'
      - '--no-net'
      - '--no-vm-kill'
      - '--safeboot'
      - '--safeboot-instance'
      - '--drag-and-drop'
      - '--extra-verbose'
  condition: selection_token and selection_chain
falsepositives:
  - Extremely low — these flag combinations are unique to the BlackCat Rust binary and do not appear in legitimate Windows tooling
level: critical
ucs.rationale: The `--access-token=<hex>` argument is BlackCat/ALPHV-specific — Microsoft, Symantec, and CISA AA23-353A all document it as a hard-coded mutex/license gate without which the Rust binary aborts. No mainstream legitimate software ships a `--access-token` + `--child` + `--paths` + `--propagated` flag combination. Unlike a generic ransomware UC keying on shadow-copy deletion (which BlackCat shares wit

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV safe-mode-with-networking pivot — bcdedit safeboot followed by service-create + forced reboot
ucs.description: Hunts BlackCat's signature defense-evasion chain: operators reboot victims into Safe Mode with Networking (where most EDR/AV agents are inactive) using `bcdedit /set {current} safeboot network`, drop a persistence service or RunOnce entry to launch the encryptor on next boot, then force a reboot. Documented by Sophos, Trend Micro, and Microsoft in 2022-2024 ALPHV intrusions; the same pattern was reused in the Change Healthcare/Optum incident (Feb 2024). Distinguishes ALPHV from Conti/LockBit which rarely touch BCD safeboot.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND ((Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe AND Processes.process="*safeboot*" AND (Processes.process="*network*" OR Processes.process="*minimal*")) OR (Processes.process_name=shutdown.exe AND Processes.process="*/r*") OR (Processes.process_name=reg.exe AND Processes.process="*SafeBoot*") OR (Processes.process_name=sc.exe AND Processes.process="*safeboot*")) by host Processes.user _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(proc) as distinct_proc_count by host | where distinct_proc_count >= 2 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let SafeBootCfg = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
              and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("network", "minimal", "{current}", "{default}"))
         or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has @"\SafeBoot\Network")
         or (FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot")
    | project ConfigTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ConfigCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ConfigParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/r", "-r"))
     or (FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/f")
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Restart-Computer","shutdown /r"))
| join kind=inner SafeBootCfg on DeviceName
| where Timestamp between (ConfigTime .. ConfigTime + WindowMin)
| project ConfigTime, RebootTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ConfigCmd, RebootCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ConfigTime),
          ConfigParent
| order by ConfigTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let SafeBootCfg = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe" and CommandLine has "safeboot"
              and CommandLine has_any ("network", "minimal", "{current}", "{default}"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\reg.exe" and CommandLine has @"\SafeBoot\Network")
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\sc.exe" and CommandLine has "safeboot")
    | project ConfigTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ConfigCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\shutdown.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("/r", "-r", "/f"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("Restart-Computer","shutdown /r"))
| join kind=inner SafeBootCfg on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (ConfigTime .. ConfigTime + WindowMin)
| project ConfigTime, RebootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ConfigCmd, RebootCmd = CommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ConfigTime)
| order by ConfigTime desc
ucs.rationale: BCD safe-boot manipulation is one of the few defense-evasion techniques BlackCat operators have institutionalised across affiliates — Microsoft's 'Many Lives of BlackCat' (June 2022) and Sophos X-Ops post-mortems on multiple ALPHV intrusions specifically call out the `bcdedit /set {current} safeboot network` → service create → reboot chain. Conti, LockBit 3.0, Royal, and BlackBasta either skip saf
